texto: Jackbaiting with spinning setup, there are a few advantages of a baitcasting setup. One is allows you to choose lighter line. Here I'm fishing braid right here. It's about 68lb fluorocarbon diameter. What's the benefit of choosing light line for jackbait? Because when jackbait become advantage or dominate in a situation where you have a lot of winds usually. Early spring, when there's a lot of wind, you go smaller in diameter of the line, less effect by wind. There you can keep the casting distance, control, less line ball and all that. And frees up the jackbait action of course. The second advantage of choosing a spinning setup for jackbaiting is the cadence. With the baitcasting setup, you're pretty much, if you're jerking with the left hand, you're pretty much limited to jerk this side of the ball, casting that direction to this way. It is very difficult to jerk this way. Good anglers can do it, but it's not very comfortable. But with the spinning, you have ability to jerk the rod any direction pretty much. And you can do upper jack when you need to. And then third advantage of spinning is the drag actually. High-end spinning reels drag is very, very smooth startup in this year. That keeps the fish pinned, because often traditionally, cold water, jackbait, like one thing silent like this. You know, the bite isn't so hard. A lot of times, those finicky fish pre-spawn, they just touch the bait. And then you continue to do your cadence, and then you don't feel the bite, but then you set the hook on your jack cadence. There, if the fish is big enough, it pulls back. But then your startup in ashe, the smooth drag of the spinning reel, will come in play to eliminate line break. And then also during the fight, inactive, like principal cold water fish, tend not to fight as aggressive as warm water season, like summertime. Versus cold water season, they're more like trying to go deeper. Fish look at your boat and make the last panic run. And then for that too, spinning drag will suit it more for that. All right, here I'm hooked up with a decent size smallie on the Vision 110 Silent Colors Math Chat, I mean the Math Chat. That is phenomenal in low light conditions, also clear water, pretty much any water condition besides super muddy. This is a pretty decent size smallmouth, but as some of you may think the spinning rod isn't strong enough, you will feel enough power there behind this Ronin. It's compact, short stick, very cadence friendly, the Chunky Smallies 110 Silent. Silent Math Chat, Ronin, Chunky Smallies. Ladies. We'll see you next time.
